concerns.text: FR-004 mandates a 'stratified 80/20 train-test split' IF N >= 80, ELSE halt. However, User Story 3 (US-3) and FR-005 define a validation strategy that switches to LOOCV for N < 100. This creates a logical contradiction: if 80 <= N < 100, FR-004 requires an 80/20 split, but FR-005/US-3 requires LOOCV. The spec does not define which rule takes precedence in this range, making the system behavior undefined.

FR-005 specifies 'LOOCV for N < 100, 5-fold CV for N >= 100'. This contradicts the 'Edge Cases' section which states the system MUST halt if N < 80. If the system halts at N < 80, the condition 'N < 100' in FR-005 effectively covers the range [80, 99]. However, the 'Edge Cases' also mention 'statistical power will be insufficient for reliable regression and valid 80/20 splitting' for N < 80, implying 80/20 is the intended split method. The spec fails to reconcile the split method (80/20 vs LOOCV) for the 80-99 range.

FR-003.1 requires applying Isometric Log-Ratio (ILR) transformation to compositional features. However, FR-003 requires calculating specific scalar descriptors (delta_radius, VEC, mixing_entropy, mixing_enthalpy). The spec does not clarify if these scalars are to be used *instead* of the ILR features, *in addition* to them, or if the ILR transformation is applied *to* the raw composition before deriving the scalars. Using both ILR coordinates (which span the simplex) and deterministic scalar functions of the same composition in the same model risks multicollinearity or data leakage, creating a contradiction in the feature engineering strategy.

FR-001 requires downloading 'Materials Project data' in addition to the MPEA database. However, the 'Assumptions' section and 'User Story 1' only reference the MPEA database (DOI 10.1038/s41597-020-00768-9). There is no defined source, access method, or filtering logic for the 'Materials Project' data mentioned in FR-001, creating a requirement that cannot be implemented based on the provided spec text.

User Story 3 mentions 'Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV) for N < 100 or 100-iteration bootstrap for N >= 100'. This contradicts FR-005 which specifies '5-fold CV for N >= 100'. The spec is inconsistent regarding the validation strategy for datasets with N >= 100 (Bootstrap vs 5-fold CV).

The Edge Cases state: 'If the dataset contains fewer than 80 BCC alloys... The system MUST flag a Data Scarcity Warning and halt'. However, FR-004 states: 'IF the dataset size is >= 80; ELSE the system MUST halt'. The Edge Cases imply a warning is issued *before* halting, while FR-004 implies an immediate halt. The exact behavior (warning vs immediate halt) is ambiguous.
